channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low, keeping intervention risks in focus
item.description: The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar since 1986 on Tuesday, keeping investors on alert for possible intervention from Japanese authorities.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed after the Dow posts new record close: Live updates
item.description: Gains in Alphabet and a pause in U.S.-Iran hostilities boosted the Dow Industrials to its first close above 52,000 on Monday.

item.title: China factory activity grows faster than expected in June on tech export demand
item.description: China's manufacturing engine held up this year as surging demand for artificial intelligence technology helped offset the drag from Middle East turmoil.

item.title: Trump bought as much as $5 million in Axon stock before ICE sought $220 million Taser deal
item.description: Axon Enterprise has ramped up lobbying in Congress on federal law enforcement technology as ICE pursues a potential $220 million Taser deal.

item.title: Australia's competition regulator takes Amazon to court over alleged unfair Prime subscription contract terms
item.description: Australia's competition regulator is taking Amazon to court, alleging its Prime contracts required subscribers to pay AU$2.99 to avoid advertising, with no option for refunds

item.title: BofA says these trades combine seasonals for 3Q, the month and the presidential cycle
item.description: Bank of America looks at seasonal trades for the third quarter, the coming month and the second year of a presidential cycle.

item.title: Eli Lilly, Regeneron among first companies selected for FDA initiative to speed review of new manufacturing facilities
item.description: Eli Lilly and Regeneron are among the first seven companies the FDA has selected for its PreCheck Pilot Program, CNBC has learned.

item.title: What's behind the Comcast breakup? Hope for a Disney-like valuation
item.description: NBCUniversal could look like Disney, which trades at 10-times price-to-earnings, one analyst says.

item.title: Wall Street sees more gains ahead for this SpaceX supplier
item.description: A slew of Wall Street analysts initiated coverage of Applied Aerospace & Defense at an outperform- or buy-equivalent rating.

item.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: U.S.-China tech rivalry heats up—in other countries
item.description: U.S. and Chinese tech companies are increasingly pursuing opportunities outside their home markets, with government policy support.

item.title: Supreme Court lets presidents fire independent regulators, rules for Trump in FTC case
item.description: The ruling allowing President Donald Trump to fire FTC Commissioner Rebecca Slaughter overturns a key precedent known as "Humphrey’s Executor."

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
item.description: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.

item.title: Apple supplier Luxshare seeks up to $3.1 billion in Hong Kong share sale
item.description: Apple supplier Luxshare Precision Industry is seeking to raise as much as $24.27 billion Hong Kong dollars ($3.1 billion) through a Hong Kong share sale.

item.title: Citi Wealth warns investors to move out of excess cash because of hot inflation
item.description: Here is where Citi Wealth would put that money to work instead of short-term instruments.

item.title: SpaceX bulls are back betting on huge, fast gains as Rocket Lab surges
Trump says he picks Sonderling to be labor secretary
item.description: President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social on Monday that he would nominate Keith Sonderling, who is acting labor secretary, to take on the role permanently, which would require Senate approval.

item.title: AeroVironment soars 19% on earnings beat, backlog grows to $1.2 billion
item.description: Dronemaker AeroVironment's autonomous system revenue beat expectations by $90 million and its backlog continued to grow.

item.title: Industry lobbyists push House committee to block a ban on Pentagon defense contractors buying back their own stock
item.description: Defense companies and their trade groups are pressing Congress not to require that the Pentagon approve stock buybacks.

item.title: Japan is raising visa fees by up to 400% for the first time in nearly 50 years. Here's why
item.description: A single entry visa will climb to 15,000 yen, from the 3,000 yen charged currently, while multiple entry visas will cost 30,000 yen, up from the 6,000 yen now.

item.title: Waymo and Uber end robotaxi pilot in Phoenix
item.description: The Waymo self-driving cars deployed in Phoenix for the Uber pilot will remain in use and make autonomous deliveries with DoorDash.

item.title: Salesforce is on an AI buying spree, but Wall Street still has its doubts
item.description: The company has announced three deals in the month of June alone.

item.title: Just graduated college? Here are 4 bank accounts to put your money before real life hits
item.description: Here are the best checking and savings accounts to grow your post-grad money while avoiding fees.

item.title: Micron's monster post-earnings rally is almost gone. Traders divided on where it goes next
item.description: The maker of memory chips slid to start the week, marking an about-face from its post-earnings surge.

item.title: Supreme Court rulings on Fed, FTC: What they mean for consumers
item.description: The Supreme Court issued a pair of highly anticipated rulings on Monday that have far-reaching implications for household finances.

item.title: Tuesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The Dow rose more than 300 points on Monday to post its first close above 52,000. Alphabet, a new addition to the 30-stock index, jumped almost 5%.

item.title: IRS says Trump Account contributions will not trigger annual gift tax reporting requirements
item.description: Parents, guardians and others who contribute to a Trump Account will not be required to file a gift tax return, the IRS and Treasury Department said Monday.

item.title: Oil prices near pre-war levels — but persistent supply risks could spark a rebound, analysts warn
item.description: Commodity strategists warn that Iranian leverage in the Strait of Hormuz will continue to weigh on oil markets and shipping costs.

item.title: Trump laments 'tremendous loss' on mail-in ballots at Supreme Court, doubles down on voter-ID bill
item.description: Justice Amy Coney Barrett, whom Trump nominated in 2020, rejected arguments that federal laws preempt Mississippi permitting late-arriving absentee ballots.

item.title: Comcast announces it will spin off NBCUniversal and Sky from cable business
item.description: Comcast said it will separate into two publicly traded companies through a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal and Sky.

item.title: Comcast's NBCUniversal spinoff raises hope for more deals. There may not be good options
item.description: Comcast plans to separate its cable and media divisions over the next year. This could set each company up for M&A, but there might not be any good options.
